properties.title: suspicious grizzly
properties.thought: gravel keeps the world going round
properties.status: i prefer gravel. to each their own

properties.title: Departure — Anchorage, AK
properties.thought: Waldo has been pointed north. The pavement ends somewhere ahead and Waldo can already feel it. KO3s are ready. Starlink dome is locked. The camper smells like fresh coffee and ambition.

properties.title: Fuel Stop — Cantwell
properties.thought: Waldo distrusts all range estimates north of the Alaska Range. The gauge said half a tank. Waldo filled it anyway. This is not pessimism. This is arithmetic.

properties.title: Denali in Full View
properties.thought: At 20,310 feet, Denali doesn't care about Waldo. And yet Waldo cannot look away. There is a humility that comes with being a small truck next to the largest thing in North America.
properties.status: Clear skies. Pulled over for 40 minutes. No regrets.

properties.title: Wild Camp — Nenana River Corridor
properties.thought: Waldo is parked on gravel 50 feet from the Nenana River. The sound is extraordinary. There is no cell signal and Starlink is pointed through a spruce canopy. This is fine. This is the point.
properties.status: Camped 2100. River: 15ft, fast. Bears: theoretically present.

properties.title: Mud Season is Real
properties.thought: The GPS said the road was passable. The road had a different opinion. Waldo did not get stuck — but Waldo had a conversation with gravity that lasted 20 minutes and involved a hi-lift jack.
properties.status: Road passable but deeply rutted. Aired down to 20psi. Recovered without assistance.
properties.photoCaption: Test image for validating the event photo popup.

properties.title: Fairbanks — First Resupply
properties.thought: Civilization detected. Waldo is wary of parking lots but will tolerate them for propane, groceries, and a shower. The KO3s are already caked with the Nenana corridor. Good.
properties.status: Fairbanks, AK. Resupply complete. Laundry: done. Morale: high.
